title: Add structured event payloads with progress percentage to Rick Terminal
source: Real-time dashboard UX patterns — progressive disclosure and granular progress feedback reduce perceived wait time by 30-40%
description: Currently roro_events.py only emits coarse lifecycle events (started/completed/failed). Rick Terminal has no visibility into what a Morty is actually doing mid-task. Users see a static 'working' state for minutes with no feedback. Adding progress events with percentage, current_step, and output_preview fields enables Rick Terminal to show live progress bars and streaming output per agent.
proposed_changes: In roro_events.py: (1) Add new event type `cto.morty.delegation.progress` with schema {percentage: 0-100, current_step: str, output_lines: int, elapsed_seconds: float}. (2) Add `emit_progress()` convenience function that rate-limits to max 1 event per 2 seconds to avoid flooding. In delegate.py: (3) While reading claude subprocess stdout, parse output lines and emit progress events every N lines or when detecting phase markers (e.g., 'Reading file', 'Writing file', 'Running tests'). This gives Rick Terminal the data it needs to render per-agent progress bars and live output previews.
